eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom commands trader consensus at 26% for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, propelled by his dominant leads in March California primary polls and New Hampshire surveys, underscoring executive experience and national fundraising strength in a fragmented field. Former Vice President Kamala Harris trails at 10% despite April national polls like McLaughlin's awarding her 29% and her April 10 remarks signaling interest in another run, hampered by 2024 general election defeat. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez garners 8% via progressive and youth appeal, while Sen. Jon Ossoff hit 6.5% on anti-Trump rhetoric before denying ambitions. Key differentiators include governors' records, Senate profiles, and ideological bases; 2026 midterms, donor backing, and early primary positioning could consolidate support.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A surge of injuries over the past two weeks has leveled the 2026 FIFA World Cup field, capping trader consensus probabilities tightly around France (16.4%) and Spain (15.3%) while denting Brazil (8.6%) and Argentina (8.6%). Key absences include Brazil's Rodrygo (torn ACL) and Éder Militão (hamstring surgery), Germany's Serge Gnabry (torn adductor), England's Jack Grealish (stress fracture), and France's Hugo Ekitike (Achilles rupture), with doubts over Argentina's Cristian Romero (knee) and Spain's Lamine Yamal (hamstring). France edges ahead via Kylian Mbappé's recovery and a favorable Group I (Senegal, Norway, playoff), Spain holds firm despite Uruguay in Group H, but the 48-team format amplifies upset risks from dark horses like Morocco in Brazil's group.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance holds a strong position as heir apparent in the 2028 Republican presidential nomination race at 39% trader consensus, but Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s 49% lead reflects his enduring populist appeal and prior alignment with Trump, keeping the field tightly contested alongside Marco Rubio's 22% amid recent surges. Mixed straw polls, including Rubio's 35% CPAC finish in late March and Vance topping youth and pro-life surveys in April, underscore no clear frontrunner two years out, with Trump distancing himself from viewing Vance as successor. Dynamics hinge on 2026 midterm performance, fundraising edges, and potential Trump endorsements, as the wide GOP primary field awaits separation via early state caucuses and primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus at 20.6% implied probability to win the 2028 presidential election, just ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17.6%, amid a highly fragmented field with over two dozen candidates and no clear frontrunner beyond 20%. This tight race stems from early-stage shadow primaries, where Vance benefits from incumbency as Republican heir apparent post-2024 and tops GOP primary polls at 43-46%, while Newsom leads Democratic nominee markets at 26-27% despite Kamala Harris's lingering support. Recent Echelon Insights polling in late April highlighted shifting contender dynamics, but 2026 midterm outcomes, potential Trump endorsements, official candidacy announcements, and economic performance could widen gaps or elevate challengers like Marco Rubio. High trading volume exceeding $550 million underscores skin-in-the-game uncertainty ahead of primaries starting in 2027.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 57.5% implied probability after clinching the NBA's best 64-18 regular-season record, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's elite scoring (33.8 PPG), Chet Holmgren's rim protection, and a franchise-record 16-game winning streak plus historic net rating, positioning them as Western Conference No. 1 seed with home-court advantage through playoffs. San Antonio Spurs trail at 20.2% with a potent 62-20 mark, driven by Victor Wembanyama's dominance and De'Aaron Fox's addition via trade, securing the No. 2 West seed. New York Knicks (8.6%) edge Eastern rivals like Detroit Pistons (3.9%) and Cleveland Cavaliers (3.5%) on depth and 53-29 finish as No. 3 seed, amid first-round series now underway with no major injuries shifting rosters. Philadelphia 76ers (3.1%) and Los Angeles Lakers (2.9%) face steeper paths from lower seeds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding 3-0 victory over Fulham on May 2 has propelled them six points clear atop the Premier League table with a superior +41 goal difference, driving trader consensus to a 62.5% implied probability for the Gunners as champions despite Manchester City's two matches in hand. The Citizens, at 38.5%, hold a recent 2-1 head-to-head edge from April 19 but have faltered with a draw against Bournemouth, allowing Arsenal to reclaim momentum entering the run-in. Both sides boast strong recent form—Arsenal unbeaten in six, City winning five of seven—but the Gunners' easier remaining fixtures against mid-table opposition and home advantage in key clashes tilt the wisdom of crowds toward Mikel Arteta's squad ending their title drought.

eventMetadata.context_description: Semifinal first-leg results have traders viewing Bayern Munich, PSG, and Arsenal as near-equals at around 30-31% implied probabilities for UEFA Champions League glory, with Atletico Madrid trailing at 8.6% after a gritty 1-1 draw versus Arsenal that leaves the aggregate score level ahead of the second leg. PSG's thrilling 5-4 home win over Bayern Munich in a nine-goal epic gives the holders a slim one-goal edge, but Bayern's Allianz Arena return and home dominance fuel comeback hopes, while Arsenal's defensive resilience—bolstered by topping the league phase unbeaten—offsets Atletico's set-piece prowess and Simeone's knockout pedigree. Momentum from quarterfinal triumphs over Real Madrid (Bayern), Sporting (Arsenal), and Barcelona (Atletico) underscores the razor-thin margins in this wide-open race.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's 34.2% implied probability leads the F1 Drivers' Championship market, reflecting his real-world points lead at 75 after strong starts, including back-to-back wins from pole in China and Japan—boasting a perfect 100% pole-to-victory rate and average finish of 1.33. Teammate George Russell trails closely at 28.5% and 68 points, buoyed by consistent qualifying (22-5 head-to-head vs. Antonelli) and podiums, but dented by recent bad luck like the Japanese GP. Mercedes' early dominance under 2026 regulations fuels the tight intra-team battle, while Verstappen (8.6%), Norris (9.5%), and Leclerc (8.3%) lag amid Red Bull and McLaren setup woes; the long 24-race calendar and Miami Grand Prix sprint keep dynamics fluid for challengers.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket crowns Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen with "Liekinheitin" as the frontrunner at 33.6% implied probability, driven by its orchestral pop flair—violin strings paired with fiery staging concepts—that promises strong jury appeal and pre-rehearsal buzz mirroring past Nordic successes like Sweden's recent wins. Greece's Akylas surges to 18.3% on the vibrant, televote-friendly "Ferto" (boosted by its April "Super Ferto" remix and London pre-party energy), while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund climbs to 12.4% via emotional ballad "Før vi går hjem" gaining post-national final traction. Australia (Delta Goodrem's star-powered "Eclipse") and France (Monroe's dynamic "Regarde!") hold mid-pack on Eurojury points and stand-in rehearsal highlights, but the wide-open field underscores rehearsal volatility ahead of semi-finals on May 12 and 14.

title: Netanyahu out by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Netanyahu out by end of 2026?

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remains in office with his right-wing coalition maintaining a slim Knesset majority after passing the 2026 state budget on March 31, averting a snap election trigger. Recent Tel Aviv protests on April 25 demanded his resignation amid accusations of security failures in ongoing conflicts with Hamas, Hezbollah, and Iran, while his April 24 disclosure of successful early-stage prostate cancer treatment has sparked health-related speculation. Opposition parties have withdrawn some no-confidence motions but continue pressing amid trailing polls for the legislative elections due by October 27. Coalition tensions over ultra-Orthodox conscription exemptions and judicial issues persist, with traders weighing stability against potential fractures or health developments ahead of the vote.

title: LALIGA Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 La Liga (soccer).

markets.question: Will Celta Vigo win the 2025–26 La Liga?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will win the 2025–26 La Liga.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 La Liga. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to win the league (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 La Liga season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from La Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding 14-point lead atop the La Liga table—88 points from 34 matches with a league-high +58 goal difference—drives trader consensus at 99.7% implied probability after their hard-fought 2-1 victory at Osasuna yesterday, where late strikes from Robert Lewandowski and Ferran Torres sealed three crucial points despite heavy rain and a resilient host. Real Madrid trail on 74 points from 33 games, requiring a perfect run through their five remaining fixtures, including today's clash at Espanyol, while Barcelona drop all 12 points from four games—an unprecedented collapse for Hansi Flick's rampant side, boasting 29 wins and defensive solidity (just 31 goals conceded). A Real Madrid slip today would mathematically clinch the title; otherwise, El Clásico next weekend offers Barça a historic coronation opportunity on the biggest stage, barring freak injuries or disqualifications.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's Tisza Party secured a commanding supermajority of 141 seats in Hungary's National Assembly following the April 12, 2026, parliamentary election, with final counts confirming the result and Viktor Orbán conceding defeat after 16 years in power. This outcome, driven by high voter turnout and widespread desire for regime change, positions Magyar as the presumptive prime minister, to be formally elected when the new parliament convenes by May 12. Trader consensus at 99.7% reflects this near-certainty, bolstered by Tisza's constitutional amendment powers and lack of viable challenges. Only extraordinary events like legal disputes over results, Magyar's withdrawal, or health issues could shift odds, though none have emerged in recent weeks.

title: NBA MVP
description: This is a market on predicting the winner of the NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP) award for the 2025–26 NBA regular season.

markets.question: Will Trae Young win the 2025–2026 NBA MVP?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Trae Young is awarded the 2025–26 regular season NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP) Award. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 NBA MVP award, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jaylen Brown is awarded the 2025–26 regular season NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP) Award. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 NBA MVP award, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Shai Gilgeous-Alexander at 97.9% implied probability for 2025-26 Kia NBA MVP after leading the Oklahoma City Thunder to a league-best 64-18 record and Western Conference top seed, capping a season of historic efficiency with 31.1 points, 6.6 assists, 55% field goal shooting, and the lowest turnovers ever in a 30+ PPG campaign. As reigning MVP and Clutch Player of the Year winner, he topped the final Kia MVP Ladder and The Athletic's player poll, outpacing surging finalist Victor Wembanyama (DPOY, Spurs' 62-20 mark) and stat-sheet dominator Nikola Jokic (league-leading rebounds/assists, Nuggets 54-28). Realistic challenges include voters prioritizing Wembanyama's two-way impact or Jokic's triple-double volume prior to official announcement.

title: Venezuela leader end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez maintains practical control of Venezuela four months after U.S. special forces captured Nicolás Maduro on January 3, 2026, assuming interim duties via Supreme Tribunal of Justice order under constitutional rules for presidential absence. Recent U.S. sanction lifts on Rodríguez since April have spurred oil flows, wage increases to $240 monthly, and diplomatic outreach to neighbors like Colombia and Barbados, bolstering regime stability despite her exceeded 90-day term and no election timeline from the National Assembly. Opposition leader María Corina Machado plans a late-2026 return while unifying support and urging polls, yet trader consensus favors Maduro's titular constitutional status (64.4%) amid stalled transition, Rodríguez's consolidation (21.5%), and Machado's long odds (7.5%).

eventMetadata.context_description: Entering the second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, trader consensus favors the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche at 31.1% implied probability after a dominant first-round sweep, bolstered by Nathan MacKinnon's offensive firepower and Cale Makar's defensive prowess, closely trailed by the Carolina Hurricanes at 24.5% following their sweep of the Ottawa Senators and stingy penalty kill. The race remains tight due to a competitive bracket, with Vegas Golden Knights leveraging 2023 championship experience, surging Buffalo Sabres ending a 15-year playoff series win drought, and Tampa Bay Lightning's veteran core still alive amid ongoing Eastern series. No major injuries reported, but short series turnarounds and road games in matchups like Avalanche-Minnesota Wild heighten upset potential across conferences.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following a fragile ceasefire on April 8 amid the 2026 Iran war—sparked by US and Israeli strikes in late February that killed top Iranian leaders—US-Iran diplomacy centers on Pakistan-mediated talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the US naval blockade imposed April 13. Iran's latest proposal, conveyed last week, offers strait access in exchange for ending the blockade but postpones nuclear constraints, drawing skepticism from President Trump, who stated on May 2 it fails to exact a sufficient price after 47 years of tensions. No date is set for the next negotiation round, with traders weighing economic pressures on Iran against US demands for verifiable limits on its nuclear and ballistic missile programs before any permanent peace agreement explicitly ending hostilities.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent AtlasIntel and Datafolha polls from late April show incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding a slim first-round lead over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro—47% to 40% in AtlasIntel—but trailing by 0.3 points in simulated runoffs ahead of the October 4 first round and potential October 25 second round. Polymarket traders reflect this razor-thin contest in their consensus, pricing Flávio Bolsonaro at 43.5% implied probability to win versus Lula's 38.5%, with fragmented support for others like Renan Santos at 5.7%. The race stays tight due to polarized voters loyal to PT incumbency versus PL's Bolsonaro legacy, high rejection rates exceeding 50% for both, and undecideds at 5-19%. Separation could come from June-August party conventions confirming candidacies, economic indicators, or early debates.

title: Will Jesus Christ return before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if The Second Coming of Jesus Christ occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

markets.question: Will Jesus Christ return before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if The Second Coming of Jesus Christ occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certain 97.7% implied probability for "No" on Jesus Christ's return before 2027, driven by the total absence of verifiable biblical signs—such as a globally visible appearance in glory, the rise of the Antichrist, or widespread tribulation—through May 2026, with seven months remaining until the December 31 deadline. Cultural chatter on social media and fringe forums speculates on timelines tied to ancient prophecies or current events like geopolitical tensions, but these remain unconfirmed and echo centuries of failed doomsday predictions, reinforcing skepticism among capital-backed bettors. Realistic upsets would require an undeniable, consensus-verified global event aligning with scriptural depictions, though historical patterns and Matthew 24:36's emphasis on unpredictability leave room for rapid shifts in viral sentiment.

title: Next French Presidential Election
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Jordan Bardella of the National Rally holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2027 French presidential first round, buoyed by his party's consistent polling leads—around 40% potential in recent Harris and Verian surveys—stemming from far-right gains in the March 2026 municipal elections and ongoing fallout from the 2024 snap legislative vote that produced a hung parliament. Édouard Philippe, former prime minister and Horizons leader, trails closely after his Le Havre mayoral reelection, positioning him as the top runoff challenger per BFMTV/OpinionWay data, appealing to centrists wary of RN dominance. The race remains tight amid a fragmented field, with Les Républicains nominating Bruno Retailleau and left-wing divisions; separation could arise from candidate declarations, economic shifts, or coalition maneuvers ahead of April 2027 voting.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori of Fuerza Popular and Roberto Sánchez Palomino of Juntos por el Perú advanced to Peru's June 7 presidential runoff after first-round results on April 12–13, with over 96% of ballots tallied showing Fujimori at 17.1% and Sánchez edging Rafael López Aliaga at 12.0%—a lead solidified by late April rural vote surges. Recent Ipsos and IEP polls depict a deadlocked matchup around 49–51%, yet trader consensus prices Fujimori a 65.5% implied probability to win, reflecting her name recognition from prior runs, robust urban and Lima support, party infrastructure advantages, and wariness of leftist policies amid Peru's decade of instability including Pedro Castillo's ouster. Sánchez's surprise rural strength sustains 31.4% odds, with López Aliaga's residual 2.3% bets on recount challenges. Official first-round certification awaits mid-May amid delays.
